title: $ $-Structure
statement:
Given a signature $\mathcal{L}$, an \textbf{$\mathcal{L}$-structure}
 $\mathcal{A}$ consists of:
 - A nonempty set $A$, called the \textbf{universe} (or
 \emph{carrier set}),
- For each constant symbol $c$: an element $c^{\mathcal{A}} \in A$,
- For each $n$-ary function symbol $f$: a function
 $f^{\mathcal{A}} : A^n \to A$,
- For each $n$-ary relation symbol $R$: a subset
 $R^{\mathcal{A}} \subseteq A^n$.
 We write $\mathcal{A} = (A, \ldots)$, listing the universe and
 interpretations.
